elements.plain_summary: The declarations page identifies the named insured and insured location, states the policy period (2026-01-01 to 2027-01-01), and lists the coverage limits, deductible, and total premium that apply to this policy.
Coverage A - Dwelling insures the house itself against direct physical loss, up to the $350,000 limit shown on the declarations and after the $1,000 deductible, subject to the Section I exclusions.

Coverage C - Personal Property covers belongings owned or used by an insured, generally up to 50% of the Coverage A limit ($175,000), with special sub-limits for categories such as jewelry and electronics.
Coverage D - Loss of Use pays additional living expenses, up to $70,000, while the home is not fit to live in because of a covered loss; it references endorsement END-99, which was not provided.
Section I - Exclusions lists perils the form does not insure, including flood, surface water, earth movement, and neglect; losses from these perils fall outside Section I coverage.

The Duties After Loss condition sets out what an insured must do after a loss - give prompt notice, protect the property from further damage, prepare an inventory, and cooperate with the investigation.
The policy defines actual cash value as the replacement cost of property reduced by depreciation for its age and condition.
Endorsement HO 04 90 adds limited water-backup coverage, up to the sub-limit shown on the declarations, for loss caused by water backing up through sewers or drains.
The premium summary shows a total annual premium of $1,842.00, allocated across the Section I property coverages, Section II liability, and the endorsements.

data_gaps: Endorsement END-99, referenced by Coverage D - Loss of Use, was not included in the provided document, so its terms could not be explained.
narrative: This explains policy ****7788, form HO-3 (07/2021), in force from 2026-01-01 to 2027-01-01. The declarations page lists the named insured, the insured location, the coverage limits, the $1,000 deductible, and the total premium. Section I provides dwelling coverage (Coverage A, $350,000), personal property coverage (Coverage C, $175,000), and loss-of-use coverage (Coverage D, $70,000), each subject to the deductible and the Section I exclusions. Section I - Exclusions lists perils the form does not insure, such as flood, surface water, and earth movement. The Duties After Loss condition describes the steps an insured takes after a loss. One referenced endorsement (END-99) was not included in the document, so its terms could not be explained. Whether any particular situation results in a payment depends on the exact policy language and the facts of the loss; contact the insurer or agent to confirm. Informational explanation only; not a coverage determination, claim decision, or insurance/legal advice.
